diagrams.title: Equivalence of Computational Models
diagrams.description: A diagram showing how different formalizations of computation (Turing Machine, Lambda Calculus, Recursive Functions, Register Machine) all compute the same class of functions - the Turing-computable functions. This visually demonstrates the Church-Turing thesis by showing multiple independent approaches yielding the same result.
elements: All pointing to common 'Turing-Computable Functions' set
Bidirectional arrows showing equivalence between all models
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates that all major formulations of computation lead to the same class of functions, supporting the Church-Turing thesis that this class captures all intuitively computable functions

diagrams.title: Decidability Spectrum
diagrams.description: A hierarchical diagram showing the spectrum of problems from decidable (solvable) to undecidable (unsolvable). Shows P ⊆ NP ⊆ PSPACE ⊆ EXPTIME within decidable problems, then the boundary with undecidable problems like the Halting Problem.
elements: Box for 'Decidable Problems' containing P, NP, PSPACE, EXPTIME
Box for 'Undecidable Problems' containing Halting Problem, Post Correspondence
Dashed boundary line between decidable and undecidable
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates what the Church-Turing thesis means in practice - that even with all computational models being equivalent, there exist problems that cannot be solved by any algorithm (undecidable)

diagrams.title: Church and Turing Convergence
diagrams.description: A conceptual diagram showing how Church's approach (lambda calculus) and Turing's approach (Turing machines) independently arrived at the same conclusion about computability, represented as two streams converging into one.
elements: Final box labeled 'Church-Turing Thesis: All computable functions are Turing-computable'
diagrams.purpose: Shows the historical convergence of two different approaches to defining computation, emphasizing that the thesis emerged from independent mathematical investigations yielding the same result
